content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be improved by reverse-engineering the human brain’s mechanism of memory as active learning, where human episodic memory is not a passive storage but a dynamically curated resource that prioritizes encoding uncertain, curiosity-triggering experiences to support future planning. This leads to the novel hypothesis that current curiosity-driven active learning agents can achieve better sample efficiency and long-horizon planning performance by adding a human-inspired gating mechanism that reactivates and updates older uncertain memory traces during offline rest periods, rather than only encoding new experiences during online interaction.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
